title: poliomintha mollis
intro: Poliomintha mollis, also known as Mexican oregano or beebrush, is a shrub-like plant native to the southwestern United States and Mexico, belonging to the mint family (Lamiaceae). Traditionally, the leaves and stems of this plant have been used in herbal medicine for their antimicrobial, antifungal, and anti-inflammatory properties, which make them effective against various health conditions. The medicinal properties of Poliomintha mollis have been linked to several health benefits, including the ability to reduce inflammation, improve respiratory issues, and support digestive health. Herbal preparations of Poliomintha mollis can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, allowing for easy incorporation into a wellness routine.
